  → No mode fired. Writing this no-op run-record and exiting cleanly.

OTHER SIDE OF THE ARGUMENT (per operator user-preference): two challenges to writing a no-op record at all —
  (1) "Don't update status.json on no-op — it just creates churn for workspace-sync." Counter: the prev_agent_run/last_analyst_run telemetry is what the decider, curmudgeon, and tinker use to detect dispatcher-shape silent-skip bugs (the same class that caused the 71-item Mode2b backlog). Writing the no-op record IS the audit trail; suppressing it is exactly what hid that bug for 14 days.
  (2) "PRED-106 closes 2026-04-30 EOM (~32h from now) — should this run pre-stage the post-closure rubric?" Counter: the 16:05Z ingestion already pre-staged the rubric in the PRED-106 expansion (silent_drop / pass-mark-via-Liboff / genuine-replication branches) and the proposal-PRED-106-ingestion.json. Restating it now adds noise. The poller will surface the closure outcome on the first post-closure poll (evening 04-30 or morning 05-01), and that triggers a new Mode 1b verdict-update via decider re-assignment — exactly the right time to write the post-closure assessment, not now.

NEXT-RUN GUIDANCE — analyst: nothing required pre-PRED-106-closure. After closure (≥2026-04-30 EOM) re-assign for verdict update per the rubric in the 16:05Z note. PRED-105 reaches d=14 past close on 2026-05-02; consider tacit_withdrawal at that point if dome remains silent.
